<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate">
            <summary>
            Represents a single streaming response chunk from an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> is so named because it represents updates
            that layer on each other to form a single agent response. Conceptually, this combines the roles of
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> and <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> in streaming output.
            </para>
            <para>
            To get the text result of this response chunk, use the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Text"/> property or simply call <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ToString"/> on the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            The relationship between <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> and <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> is
            codified in the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.ToAgentResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and
            <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ToAgentResponseUpdates"/>, which enable bidirectional conversions
            between the two. Note, however, that the provided conversions may be lossy, for example if multiple
            updates all have different <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.RawRepresentation"/> objects whereas there's only one slot for
            such an object available in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.RawRepresentation"/>.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate._contents">
            <summary>The response update content items.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.#ctor">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> class.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.#ctor(System.Nullable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatRole},System.String)">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> class.</summary>
            <param name="role">The role of the author of the update.</param>
            <param name="content">The text content of the update.</param>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.#ctor(System.Nullable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatRole},System.Collections.Generic.IList{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent})">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> class.</summary>
            <param name="role">The role of the author of the update.</param>
            <param name="contents">The contents of the update.</param>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate)">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> class.</summary>
            <param name="chatResponseUpdate">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate"/> from which to seed this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/>.</param>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.AuthorName">
            <summary>Gets or sets the name of the author of the response update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Role">
            <summary>Gets or sets the role of the author of the response update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Text">
            <summary>Gets the text of this update.</summary>
            <remarks>
            This property concatenates the text of all <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.TextContent"/> objects in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Contents"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Contents">
            <summary>Gets or sets the agent run response update content items.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.RawRepresentation">
            <summary>Gets or sets the raw representation of the response update from an underlying implementation.</summary>
            <remarks>
            If a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> is created to represent some underlying object from another object
            model, this property can be used to store that original object. This can be useful for debugging or
            for enabling a consumer to access the underlying object model if needed.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.AdditionalProperties">
            <summary>Gets or sets additional properties for the update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.AgentId">
            <summary>Gets or sets the ID of the agent that produced the response.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ResponseId">
            <summary>Gets or sets the ID of the response of which this update is a part.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.MessageId">
             <summary>Gets or sets the ID of the message of which this update is a part.</summary>
             <remarks>
             A single streaming response may be composed of multiple messages, each of which may be represented
             by multiple updates. This property is used to group those updates together into messages.
            
             Some providers may consider streaming responses to be a single message, and in that case
             the value of this property may be the same as the response ID.
            
             This value is used when <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.ToAgentResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             groups <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instances into <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> instances.
             The value must be unique to each call to the underlying provider, and must be shared by
             all updates that are part of the same logical message within a streaming response.
             </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.CreatedAt">
            <summary>Gets or sets a timestamp for the response update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ContinuationToken">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the continuation token for resuming the streamed agent response of which this update is a part.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> implementations that support background responses will return
            a continuation token on each update if background responses are allowed in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses"/>
            except for the last update, for which the token will be <see langword="null"/>.
            <para>
            This property should be used for stream resumption, where the continuation token of the latest received update should be
            passed to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ContinuationToken"/> on subsequent calls to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            to resume streaming from the point of interruption.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.FinishReason">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the reason for the agent response finishing.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatFinishReason"/> value indicating why the response finished (e.g., stop, length, content filter, tool calls),
            or <see langword="null"/> if the finish reason is not available or not yet determined (mid-stream).
            </value>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ToString">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ContentForDebuggerDisplay">
            <summary>Gets a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/> object to display in the debugger display.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.EllipsesForDebuggerDisplay">
            <summary>Gets an indication for the debugger display of whether there's more content.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1">
            <summary>
            Represents the response of the specified type <typeparamref name="T"/> to an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> run request.
            </summary>
            <typeparam name="T">The type of value expected from the agent.</typeparam>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1"/> class.
            </summary>
            <param name="response">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> from which to populate this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1"/>.</param>
            <param name="serializerOptions">The <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> to use when deserializing the result.</param>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="serializerOptions"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1.IsWrappedInObject">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether the JSON schema has an extra object wrapper.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            The wrapper is required for any non-JSON-object-typed values such as numbers, enum values, and arrays.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1.Result">
            <summary>
            Gets the result value of the agent response as an instance of <typeparamref name="T"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunContext">
            <summary>Provides context for an in-flight agent run.</summary>
        </member>

        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Role">
            <summary>Gets or sets the role of the author of the response update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Text">
            <summary>Gets the text of this update.</summary>
            <remarks>
            This property concatenates the text of all <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.TextContent"/> objects in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Contents"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.Contents">
            <summary>Gets or sets the agent run response update content items.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.RawRepresentation">
            <summary>Gets or sets the raw representation of the response update from an underlying implementation.</summary>
            <remarks>
            If a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> is created to represent some underlying object from another object
            model, this property can be used to store that original object. This can be useful for debugging or
            for enabling a consumer to access the underlying object model if needed.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.AdditionalProperties">
            <summary>Gets or sets additional properties for the update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.AgentId">
            <summary>Gets or sets the ID of the agent that produced the response.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ResponseId">
            <summary>Gets or sets the ID of the response of which this update is a part.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.MessageId">
             <summary>Gets or sets the ID of the message of which this update is a part.</summary>
             <remarks>
             A single streaming response may be composed of multiple messages, each of which may be represented
             by multiple updates. This property is used to group those updates together into messages.
            
             Some providers may consider streaming responses to be a single message, and in that case
             the value of this property may be the same as the response ID.
            
             This value is used when <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.ToAgentResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             groups <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instances into <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> instances.
             The value must be unique to each call to the underlying provider, and must be shared by
             all updates that are part of the same logical message within a streaming response.
             </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.CreatedAt">
            <summary>Gets or sets a timestamp for the response update.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ContinuationToken">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the continuation token for resuming the streamed agent response of which this update is a part.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> implementations that support background responses will return
            a continuation token on each update if background responses are allowed in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses"/>
            except for the last update, for which the token will be <see langword="null"/>.
            <para>
            This property should be used for stream resumption, where the continuation token of the latest received update should be
            passed to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ContinuationToken"/> on subsequent calls to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            to resume streaming from the point of interruption.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.FinishReason">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the reason for the agent response finishing.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatFinishReason"/> value indicating why the response finished (e.g., stop, length, content filter, tool calls),
            or <see langword="null"/> if the finish reason is not available or not yet determined (mid-stream).
            </value>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ToString">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ContentForDebuggerDisplay">
            <summary>Gets a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/> object to display in the debugger display.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.EllipsesForDebuggerDisplay">
            <summary>Gets an indication for the debugger display of whether there's more content.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1">
            <summary>
            Represents the response of the specified type <typeparamref name="T"/> to an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> run request.
            </summary>
            <typeparam name="T">The type of value expected from the agent.</typeparam>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1"/> class.
            </summary>
            <param name="response">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> from which to populate this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1"/>.</param>
            <param name="serializerOptions">The <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> to use when deserializing the result.</param>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="serializerOptions"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1.IsWrappedInObject">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether the JSON schema has an extra object wrapper.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            The wrapper is required for any non-JSON-object-typed values such as numbers, enum values, and arrays.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse`1.Result">
            <summary>
            Gets the result value of the agent response as an instance of <typeparamref name="T"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunContext">
            <summary>Provides context for an in-flight agent run.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunContext.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunContext"/> class.
            </summary>
            <param name="agent">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> that is executing the current run.</param>

            <param name="requestMessages">The request messages passed into the current run.</param>
            <param name="agentRunOptions">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> that was passed to the current run.</param>

<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse">
            <summary>
            Represents the response to an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> run request, containing messages and metadata about the interaction.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> provides one or more response messages and metadata about the response.
            A typical response will contain a single message, however a response may contain multiple messages
            in a variety of scenarios. For example, if the agent internally invokes functions or tools, performs
            RAG retrievals or has other complex logic, a single run by the agent may produce many messages showing
            the intermediate progress that the agent made towards producing the agent result.
            </para>
            <para>
            To get the text result of the response, use the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Text"/> property or simply call <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ToString"/> on the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/>.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse._messages">
            <summary>The response messages.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.#ctor">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> class.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> class.</summary>
            <param name="message">The response message to include in this response.</param>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="message"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> class from an existing <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/>.
            </summary>
            <param name="response">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/> from which to populate this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/>.</param>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="response"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            This constructor creates an agent response that wraps an existing <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/>, preserving all
            metadata and storing the original response in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.RawRepresentation"/> for access to
            the underlying implementation details.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> class from an existing <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/>.
            </summary>
            <param name="response">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> from which to copy properties.</param>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="response"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            This constructor creates a copy of an existing agent response, preserving all
            metadata and storing the original response in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.RawRepresentation"/> for access to
            the underlying implementation details.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IList{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage})">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> class with the specified collection of messages.
            </summary>
            <param name="messages">The collection of response messages, or <see langword="null"/> to create an empty response.</param>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Messages">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the collection of messages to be represented by this response.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A collection of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> instances representing the agent's response.
            If the backing collection is <see langword="null"/>, accessing this property will create an empty list.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This property provides access to all messages generated during the agent's execution. While most
            responses contain a single assistant message, complex agent behaviors may produce multiple messages
            showing intermediate steps, function calls, or different types of content.
            </para>
            <para>
            The collection is mutable and can be modified after creation. Setting this property to <see langword="null"/>
            will cause subsequent access to return an empty list.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Text">
            <summary>
            Gets the concatenated text content of all messages in this response.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A string containing the combined text from all <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.TextContent"/> instances
            across all messages in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Messages"/>, or an empty string if no text content is present.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            This property provides a convenient way to access the textual response without needing to
            iterate through individual messages and content items. Non-text content is ignored.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.AgentId">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the identifier of the agent that generated this response.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A unique string identifier for the agent, or <see langword="null"/> if not specified.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            This identifier helps track which agent generated the response in multi-agent scenarios
            or for debugging and telemetry purposes.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ResponseId">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the unique identifier for this specific response.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A unique string identifier for this response instance, or <see langword="null"/> if not assigned.
            </value>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ContinuationToken">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the continuation token for getting the result of a background agent response.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> implementations that support background responses will return
            a continuation token if background responses are allowed in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses"/>
            and the result of the response has not been obtained yet. If the response has completed and the result has been obtained,
            the token will be <see langword="null"/>.
            <para>
            This property should be used in conjunction with <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ContinuationToken"/> to
            continue to poll for the completion of the response. Pass this token to
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ContinuationToken"/> on subsequent calls to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            to poll for completion.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.CreatedAt">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the timestamp indicating when this response was created.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A <see cref="T:System.DateTimeOffset"/> representing when the response was generated,
            or <see langword="null"/> if not specified.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            The creation timestamp is useful for auditing, logging, and understanding
            the chronology of agentic interactions.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.FinishReason">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the reason for the agent response finishing.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatFinishReason"/> value indicating why the response finished (e.g., stop, length, content filter, tool calls),
            or <see langword="null"/> if the finish reason is not available.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This property is particularly useful for detecting non-normal completions, such as content filtering
            or token limit truncation, which may require special handling by the caller.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Usage">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the resource usage information for generating this response.
            </summary>
            <value>
            A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageDetails"/> instance containing token counts and other usage metrics,
            or <see langword="null"/> if usage information is not available.
            </value>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.RawRepresentation">
            <summary>Gets or sets the raw representation of the run response from an underlying implementation.</summary>
            <remarks>
            If a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> is created to represent some underlying object from another object
            model, this property can be used to store that original object. This can be useful for debugging or
            for enabling a consumer to access the underlying object model if needed.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.AdditionalProperties">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets additional properties associated with this response.
            </summary>
            <value>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AdditionalPropertiesDictionary"/> containing custom properties,
            or <see langword="null"/> if no additional properties are present.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            Additional properties provide a way to include custom metadata or provider-specific
            information that doesn't fit into the standard response schema. This is useful for
            preserving implementation-specific details or extending the response with custom data.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ToString">
            <inheritdoc />
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ToAgentResponseUpdates">
            <summary>
            Converts this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> into a collection of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instances
            suitable for streaming scenarios.
            </summary>
            <returns>
            An array of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instances that collectively represent
            the same information as this response.
            </returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method is useful for converting complete responses back into streaming format,
            which may be needed for scenarios that require uniform handling of both streaming
            and non-streaming agent responses.
            </para>
            <para>
            Each message in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Messages"/> becomes a separate update, and usage information
            is included as an additional update if present. The order of updates preserves the
            original message sequence.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides extension methods for working with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> and <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instances.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.AsChatResponse(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse)">
            <summary>
            Creates a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/> from an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> instance.
            </summary>
            <param name="response">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse"/> to convert.</param>
            <returns>A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/> built from the specified <paramref name="response"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="response"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            If the <paramref name="response"/>'s <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.RawRepresentation"/> is already a
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/> instance, that instance is returned directly.
            Otherwise, a new <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponse"/> is created and populated with the data from the <paramref name="response"/>.
            The resulting instance is a shallow copy; any reference-type members (e.g. <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.Messages"/>)
            will be shared between the two instances.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.AsChatResponseUpdate(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate)">
            <summary>
            Creates a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate"/> from an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> instance.
            </summary>
            <param name="responseUpdate">The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate"/> to convert.</param>
            <returns>A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate"/> built from the specified <paramref name="responseUpdate"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="responseUpdate"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            If the <paramref name="responseUpdate"/>'s <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.RawRepresentation"/> is already a
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate"/> instance, that instance is returned directly.
            Otherwise, a new <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseUpdate"/> is created and populated with the data from the <paramref name="responseUpdate"/>.

            <summary>

        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ContinuationToken">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the continuation token for resuming and getting the result of the agent response identified by this token.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This property is used for background responses that can be activated via the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses"/>
            property if the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> implementation supports them.
            Streamed background responses, such as those returned by default by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            can be resumed if interrupted. This means that a continuation token obtained from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ContinuationToken"/>
            of an update just before the interruption occurred can be passed to this property to resume the stream from the point of interruption.
            Non-streamed background responses, such as those returned by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>,
            can be polled for completion by obtaining the token from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ContinuationToken"/> property
            and passing it via this property on subsequent calls to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
            and polled for completion by non-streaming APIs.
            </para>
            <para>
            When this property is set to true, non-streaming APIs may start a background operation and return an initial
            response with a continuation token. Subsequent calls to the same API should be made in a polling manner with
            the continuation token to get the final result of the operation.
            </para>
            <para>
            When this property is set to true, streaming APIs may also start a background operation and begin streaming
            response updates until the operation is completed. If the streaming connection is interrupted, the
            continuation token obtained from the last update that has one should be supplied to a subsequent call to the same streaming API
            to resume the stream from the point of interruption and continue receiving updates until the operation is completed.
            </para>
            <para>
            This property only takes effect if the implementation it's used with supports background responses.
            If the implementation does not support background responses, this property will be ignored.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>

            <summary>
            Gets or sets additional properties associated with these options.
            </summary>
            <value>

            or <see langword="null"/> if no additional properties are present.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            Additional properties provide a way to include custom metadata or provider-specific
            information that doesn't fit into the standard options schema. This is useful for
            preserving implementation-specific details or extending the options with custom data.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ResponseFormat">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the response format.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            If <see langword="null"/>, no response format is specified and the agent will use its default.
            This property can be set to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Text"/> to specify that the response should be unstructured text,
            to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Json"/> to specify that the response should be structured JSON data, or
            an instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormatJson"/> constructed with a specific JSON schema to request that the
            response be structured JSON data according to that schema. It is up to the agent implementation if or how
            to honor the request. If the agent implementation doesn't recognize the specific kind of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat"/>,
            it can be ignored.
            </remarks>
        </member>

        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
            and polled for completion by non-streaming APIs.
            </para>
            <para>
            When this property is set to true, non-streaming APIs may start a background operation and return an initial
            response with a continuation token. Subsequent calls to the same API should be made in a polling manner with
            the continuation token to get the final result of the operation.
            </para>
            <para>
            When this property is set to true, streaming APIs may also start a background operation and begin streaming
            response updates until the operation is completed. If the streaming connection is interrupted, the
            continuation token obtained from the last update that has one should be supplied to a subsequent call to the same streaming API
            to resume the stream from the point of interruption and continue receiving updates until the operation is completed.
            </para>
            <para>
            This property only takes effect if the implementation it's used with supports background responses.
            If the implementation does not support background responses, this property will be ignored.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AdditionalProperties">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets additional properties associated with these options.
            </summary>
            <value>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AdditionalPropertiesDictionary"/> containing custom properties,
            or <see langword="null"/> if no additional properties are present.
            </value>
            <remarks>
            Additional properties provide a way to include custom metadata or provider-specific
            information that doesn't fit into the standard options schema. This is useful for
            preserving implementation-specific details or extending the options with custom data.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ResponseFormat">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the response format.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            If <see langword="null"/>, no response format is specified and the agent will use its default.
            This property can be set to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Text"/> to specify that the response should be unstructured text,
            to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Json"/> to specify that the response should be structured JSON data, or
            an instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormatJson"/> constructed with a specific JSON schema to request that the
            response be structured JSON data according to that schema. It is up to the agent implementation if or how
            to honor the request. If the agent implementation doesn't recognize the specific kind of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat"/>,
            it can be ignored.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone">
            <summary>
            Produces a clone of the current <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> instance.
            </summary>
            <returns>
            A clone of the current <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> instance.
            </returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The clone will have the same values for all properties as the original instance. Any collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AdditionalProperties"/>,
            are shallow-cloned, meaning a new collection instance is created, but any references contained by the collections are shared with the original.
            </para>
            <para>
            Derived types should override <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone"/> to return an instance of the derived type.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession">
            <summary>
            Base abstraction for all agent threads.
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content:
Perfect! Based on my exploration of the NuGet package XML documentation files, here's the detailed information about those classes:

## NuGet Package Analysis: microsoft.agents.ai

I found comprehensive XML documentation in both the main `microsoft.agents.ai` and `microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions` packages (version 1.0.0).

---

### 1. **AgentSession**
📍 **Location:** `microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions` package

**Constructors:**
- `AgentSession()` - Default parameterless constructor
- `AgentSession(AgentSessionStateBag)` - Constructor that accepts a state bag for initialization

**Key Details:**
- Does **NOT** take a list of `ChatMessage` in its constructor
- Contains the conversation state for a specific conversation with an agent
- Can hold conversation history, memories, or references to externally stored state
- Can be serialized/deserialized to persist across application restarts
- The `StateBag` property stores arbitrary state that persists when serialized
- Provides `GetService(Type, serviceKey)` and `GetService<TService>(serviceKey)` methods to retrieve services

---

### 2. **AgentResponse**
📍 **Location:** `microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions` package

**Key Properties:**
- **`Messages`** - Gets/sets collection of `ChatMessage` instances (the response messages)
- **`Text`** - Gets the concatenated text content of all messages (read-only property)
  - Concatenates text from all `TextContent` instances across all messages
  - Returns empty string if no text content is present
- `AgentId` - Identifier of the agent that generated the response
- `ResponseId` - Unique identifier for this response
- `FinishReason` - Reason the response finished (stop, length, content filter, tool calls, etc.)
- `Usage` - Resource usage information (token counts)
- `CreatedAt` - Timestamp when response was created
- `RawRepresentation` - Stores the underlying implementation object
- `AdditionalProperties` - Custom metadata dictionary
- `ContinuationToken` - For resuming background responses

**Constructors:**
- `AgentResponse()` - Empty response
- `AgentResponse(ChatMessage)` - Single message
- `AgentResponse(ChatResponse)` - From existing ChatResponse
- `AgentResponse(AgentResponse)` - Copy constructor
- `AgentResponse(IList<ChatMessage>)` - From message list

**To get text:** Use the `Text` property or call `ToString()`

---

### 3. **AgentResponseUpdate**
📍 **Location:** `microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions` package

**Streaming Properties:**
- **`Role`** - Gets/sets the role of the response author (ChatRole)
- **`AuthorName`** - Gets/sets the name of the response author
- **`Text`** - Gets the text of this update (read-only, concatenates text from Contents)
- **`Contents`** - Gets/sets the response content items (list of AIContent)
- **`MessageId`** - Gets/sets the ID of the message this update is part of
- **`ResponseId`** - Gets/sets the ID of the response this update is part of
- **`AgentId`** - Gets/sets the agent that produced this update
- **`ContinuationToken`** - Token for resuming interrupted streams
- **`FinishReason`** - Reason streaming finished (stop, length, content filter, etc.)
- **`CreatedAt`** - Timestamp for the update
- `RawRepresentation` - Underlying implementation object
- `AdditionalProperties` - Custom metadata

**Constructors:**
- `AgentResponseUpdate()` - Empty
- `AgentResponseUpdate(ChatRole?, string)` - Role and text content
- `AgentResponseUpdate(ChatRole?, IList<AIContent>)` - Role and content items
- `AgentResponseUpdate(ChatResponseUpdate)` - From ChatResponseUpdate

---

### 4. **ChatClientAgentOptions**
📍 **Location:** `microsoft.agents.ai` package (main package)

**Properties:**
- **`Id`** - Gets/sets the agent ID
- **`Name`** - Gets/sets the agent name
- **`Description`** - Gets/sets the agent description
- **`ChatOptions`** - Gets/sets default chat options for this agent
- **`ChatHistoryProvider`** - Gets/sets the provider for chat history
- **`AIContextProviders`** - Gets/sets list of context providers for additional context
- **`UseProvidedChatClientAsIs`** - Controls whether to apply default decorators (bool)
- **`ClearOnChatHistoryProviderConflict`** - Clear provider if service manages history (default: true)
- **`WarnOnChatHistoryProviderConflict`** - Log warning on conflict (default: true)
- **`ThrowOnChatHistoryProviderConflict`** - Throw exception on conflict (default: true)
- **`RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence`** - Persist history after each service call (default: false)

**Methods:**
- `Clone()` - Creates a new instance with same values

**Note:** There is **NO `MaxIterations`** property in `ChatClientAgentOptions`. Control of iterations appears to be part of the agent's internal logic rather than a configuration option in this class. The base `AgentRunOptions` class also doesn't define `MaxIterations`.

---

## Summary

| Class | Package | Constructor Input | Getting Text |
|-------|---------|-------------------|--------------|
| **AgentSession** | Abstractions | `AgentSessionStateBag` (optional) - NOT ChatMessage list | N/A |
| **AgentResponse** | Abstractions | `ChatMessage`, `ChatResponse`, list of `ChatMessage`, or copy constructor | `.Text` property |
| **AgentResponseUpdate** | Abstractions | `ChatRole`, text/contents, or `ChatResponseUpdate` | `.Text` property |
| **ChatClientAgentOptions** | Main | N/A (configuration class) | N/A |

3. `AgentResponseUpdate` - What properties does it have for streaming?
4. `ChatClientAgentOptions` - What properties does it have? Does it have `MaxIterations` or similar?

Look at XML documentation files in the package folders. Check both microsoft.agents.ai and microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions packages.

1. Create tools: WebSearchTool (simulated) and NoteTakingTool
2. Set up the ChatClientAgent with these tools
3. Run the agent with a research topic

- `ChatClientAgent(chatClient, instructions, name, description, tools)` 
- `agent.RunAsync(message, session, options, cancellationToken)`
- `AIFunctionFactory.Create(delegate)` to create tools from methods
- `AgentSession()` for conversation state


- `AgentResponse.Text` to extract the text output

Let me implement this now.

arguments.prompt:
I have two build errors in a .NET 10 project at /tmp/sv-9309e592a9f841d8966f43317bfd5a84/ResearchAgent that uses:
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI
- Microsoft.Agents.AI (prerelease)
- OpenAI

Error 1: `'IServiceProvider' does not contain a definition for 'UseOpenAIChatClient'`
Error 2: `Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface 'AgentSession'`

Please investigate:

1. In ~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.openai/, find the correct extension method names for registering an OpenAI chat client. Look at the XML docs. Specifically: what is the correct way to call `AddChatClient` with an OpenAI provider? What are the extension methods on the pipeline builder?

2. In ~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/ and microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/, find:
   - Is `AgentSession` abstract? What concrete class should I use instead?
   - Is there a `ChatClientAgentSession` or similar?
   - Search for all classes that inherit from or implement AgentSession

Check XML doc files and also try: `strings <dll> | grep -i session` to find session-related types.

data.reasoningText:
Two issues:
1. `UseOpenAIChatClient` - I need to check the correct API for registering OpenAI chat client with DI
2. `AgentSession` is abstract - I need to find the concrete implementation

Let me investigate both.

data.content:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive answer:

## Investigation Results

### **Error 1: `'IServiceProvider' does not contain a definition for 'UseOpenAIChatClient'`**

**Root Cause:** The method doesn't exist. The code is trying to call `UseOpenAIChatClient()` on an `IServiceProvider`, which is incorrect.

**Correct Approach:**
You should use the `AsIChatClient()` extension method from `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAIClientExtensions` to wrap the OpenAI `ChatClient`, then use the builder pattern. 

**Correct Code Pattern:**

```csharp
services.AddChatClient(services =>
    new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient(modelId, apiKey).AsIChatClient())
    .Use(new OpenAI.OpenAIClient(apiKey));  // for client-level configuration
```

**Or using ChatClientBuilder:**

```csharp
var openaiClient = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient(modelId, apiKey).AsIChatClient();
var client = new ChatClientBuilder(openaiClient)
    .Use(/* middleware */)
    .Build();
services.AddSingleton<IChatClient>(client);
```

**Available extension methods on ChatClientBuilder** (from Microsoft.Extensions.AI v10.4.0):
- `.Use()` - for custom middleware/decorators
- `.UseDistributedCache()` - for response caching
- `.UseFunctionInvocation()` - for tool calling
- `.UseLogging()` - for logging
- `.UseOpenTelemetry()` - for tracing
- `.UseChatReducer()` - for history compression
- `.ConfigureOptions()` - for default options
- `.AsBuilder()` - convert IChatClient to builder

The `AsIChatClient()` methods available in `OpenAIClientExtensions`:
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient)` ✅
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Responses.ResponsesClient)` - for Responses API
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient, string, string)` - for Assistants
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient, Assistant, string)` - for Assistants

---

### **Error 2: `Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface 'AgentSession'`**

**Root Cause:** `AgentSession` is abstract (base abstraction for all agent sessions) and cannot be instantiated directly.

**Solution:** Use **`ChatClientAgentSession`** instead, which is the concrete implementation for use with `ChatClientAgent`.

**Classes Found:**

| Type | Namespace | Status | Purpose |
|------|-----------|--------|---------|
| `AgentSession` | `Microsoft.Agents.AI` | **ABSTRACT** | Base abstraction for all agent sessions |
| **`ChatClientAgentSession`** | `Microsoft.Agents.AI` | **CONCRETE** ✅ | Concrete implementation for `ChatClientAgent` |

**Correct Code:**

```csharp
// WRONG:
var session = new AgentSession();  // ❌ Cannot instantiate abstract class

// CORRECT:
var session = new ChatClientAgentSession();  // ✅
```

---

### **Summary of Fixes**

**Line 17 (Error 1):** Change from:
```csharp
services.AddChatClient(b => b.UseOpenAIChatClient(modelId, new OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions {...}).Use(...))
```

To:
```csharp
services.AddChatClient(services =>
    new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient(modelId, apiKey).AsIChatClient())
```

**Line 70 (Error 2):** Change from:
```csharp
var session = new AgentSession();
```

To:
```csharp
var session = new ChatClientAgentSession();
```

```csharp
services.AddChatClient(services =>
    new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient(modelId, apiKey).AsIChatClient())
    .Use(new OpenAI.OpenAIClient(apiKey));  // for client-level configuration
```

**Or using ChatClientBuilder:**

```csharp
var openaiClient = new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient(modelId, apiKey).AsIChatClient();
var client = new ChatClientBuilder(openaiClient)
    .Use(/* middleware */)
    .Build();
services.AddSingleton<IChatClient>(client);
```

**Available extension methods on ChatClientBuilder** (from Microsoft.Extensions.AI v10.4.0):
- `.Use()` - for custom middleware/decorators
- `.UseDistributedCache()` - for response caching
- `.UseFunctionInvocation()` - for tool calling
- `.UseLogging()` - for logging
- `.UseOpenTelemetry()` - for tracing
- `.UseChatReducer()` - for history compression
- `.ConfigureOptions()` - for default options
- `.AsBuilder()` - convert IChatClient to builder

The `AsIChatClient()` methods available in `OpenAIClientExtensions`:
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient)` ✅
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Responses.ResponsesClient)` - for Responses API
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient, string, string)` - for Assistants
- `AsIChatClient(OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient, Assistant, string)` - for Assistants

### **Error 2: `Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface 'AgentSession'`**

**Root Cause:** `AgentSession` is abstract (base abstraction for all agent sessions) and cannot be instantiated directly.

**Solution:** Use **`ChatClientAgentSession`** instead, which is the concrete implementation for use with `ChatClientAgent`.

**Classes Found:**

| Type | Namespace | Status | Purpose |
|------|-----------|--------|---------|
| `AgentSession` | `Microsoft.Agents.AI` | **ABSTRACT** | Base abstraction for all agent sessions |
| **`ChatClientAgentSession`** | `Microsoft.Agents.AI` | **CONCRETE** ✅ | Concrete implementation for `ChatClientAgent` |

**Correct Code:**

```csharp
// WRONG:
var session = new AgentSession();  // ❌ Cannot instantiate abstract class

// CORRECT:
var session = new ChatClientAgentSession();  // ✅
```

---

### **Summary of Fixes**

**Line 17 (Error 1):** Change from:
```csharp
services.AddChatClient(b => b.UseOpenAIChatClient(modelId, new OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions {...}).Use(...))
```

To:
```csharp
services.AddChatClient(services =>
    new OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient(modelId, apiKey).AsIChatClient())
```

**Line 70 (Error 2):** Change from:
```csharp
var session = new AgentSession();
```

To:
```csharp
var session = new ChatClientAgentSession();
```

arguments.name: fix-api-issues
arguments.prompt:
I have 3 build errors in a .NET 10 project. Please investigate the NuGet packages at ~/.nuget/packages/ to find the correct API:

**Error 1**: `cannot convert from 'string' to 'System.ClientModel.ApiKeyCredential'` when calling `new OpenAI.OpenAIClient(apiKey, options)` where apiKey is a string.
- Check ~/.nuget/packages/openai/ for the OpenAIClient constructor signatures. Does it need `new ApiKeyCredential(apiKey)` or similar?

**Error 2**: `'IServiceProvider' does not contain a definition for 'Use'` - The AddChatClient builder callback receives an IServiceProvider, not a ChatClientBuilder.
- Check ~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai/ for the `AddChatClient` extension method signatures. What type does the lambda receive? How to properly chain the OpenAI chat client?
- Also check microsoft.extensions.ai.openai for AddChatClient or AddOpenAIChatClient extension methods.

**Error 3**: `'ChatClientAgentSession' does not contain a constructor that takes 0 arguments`
- Check ~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/ for ChatClientAgentSession constructors. What parameters are required?

Look at XML doc files in the lib/net10.0/ or lib/net9.0/ folders of each package. Also try `strings <dll> | grep -i 'AddChatClient\|AddOpenAI\|ChatClientAgentSession'` on the relevant DLLs.

            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession"/> class.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the ID of the underlying service chat history to support cases where the chat history is stored by the agent service.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This property may be null in the following cases:
            <list type="bullet">
            <item><description>The agent stores messages via a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatHistoryProvider"/> and not in the agent service.</description></item>
            <item><description>This session object is new and server managed chat history has not yet been created in the agent service.</description></item>
            </list>
            </para>
            <para>
            The id may also change over time where the id is pointing at
            agent service managed chat history, and the default behavior of a service is
            to fork the chat history with each iteration.
